_provenance: [DESIGN / seeded 2026-07-16] Teleportal name -> tile binding registry per MD/TELEPORTAL.md §4.2 (the JSON-equivalent of the LOST per-sector .MSG '#'-square-functions). Seeded ONLY with verified/recorded bindings; every unknown field is JSON null = OPEN, which consumers MUST treat as 'unknown -> refuse honestly', never a default. Jump DESTINATIONS lived in the unrecovered content layer (SEC object byte-5 == 0x00 on every surviving teleportal cell; the authored .MSG/.CRT companions are LOST) and remain null for all entries. All teleportals keys are validated against reference/dumps/teleporter_names.json valid_names (353 VERIFIED-OBSERVED live-server names).

_schema.sector_base: str|null  SEC base name (key into world_map.json sectors); null = OPEN
_schema.x: int|null  intra-sector col 0..31 (col 0 = WEST); null = OPEN
_schema.y: int|null  intra-sector row 0..31 (row 0 = NORTH); null = OPEN
_schema.world: {x,y,layer}|null  the same tile in mra_stub.py WorldMap world coords (convenience; derived mechanically from sector_base+x+y via world_map.json, ready for player.x/player.y/player.layer assignment)
_schema.subtype: int|null  SEC object byte-1 in {1,2,3,4} (1=Teleportal 2=Room 3=Instant 4=Invisible, GT §13b)
_schema.level_req: int|null  gameplay:travel level gate; null = none documented
_schema.dest: {sector_base,x,y,layer}|null  JUMP target; null = OPEN/LOST (.CRT/.MSG content layer)
_schema._evidence: str  human-facing provenance for the row; engine ignores underscore-prefixed keys

HAV_CHURCH._evidence: [DERIVED binding / VERIFIED tile] RE-PINNED 2026-07-19 for the NEW FINAL world map (world_map.json 310-sector layout + updated MAPS). Owner walk-recorded MEMORIZE on the Haven church teleportal at world(456,379,b). SAME physical cell as before -- sector EWGB162193b intra (col=8,row=25), byte-VERIFIED via the stub WorldMap: MAPS/EWGB162193b.SEC offset 4998 = 10 01 00 00 00 00 (b1=0x01 TELEPORTAL, b5=0x00); only the ABSOLUTE world coord moved because the new map places EWGB162193b at block 14,11 (was 3,5). Pre-new-map coord was world(104,187,b). Name HAV_CHURCH VERIFIED-OBSERVED (all 3 live dumps); name<->tile DERIVED (owner report + byte). This is the recall_destination, so RECALL lands here.

HAV_INN._evidence: [DERIVED binding / VERIFIED tile] RE-PINNED 2026-07-19 on the NEW final world map. Owner walk-recorded MEMORIZE on the Haven inn teleportal at world(451,392,b). SAME physical cell as the old-map pin -- sector EWGB194225b intra (col=3,row=6), byte-VERIFIED via the stub WorldMap: raw rec 17 01 e0 00 00 00 (b1=0x01 TELEPORTAL, b5=0x00), identical bytes to the old map; only the ABSOLUTE world coord moved (sector repositioned to block 14,12; was world(99,200,b) on the 117-sector map). The pre-re-record diagnostic guess of world(451,392,b) matched exactly -- confirming EWGB194225b's sector+intra was stable across the map change (unlike KOFOR/HAV_COM's DQEV194225b, which was removed). Name HAV_INN VERIFIED-OBSERVED; name<->tile DERIVED (owner report + byte).

KOFOR._evidence: [DERIVED binding / VERIFIED tile] RE-PINNED 2026-07-19 on the NEW final world map. Owner walk-recorded MEMORIZE on the KOFOR teleportal at world(437,399,b). Byte-VERIFIED via the stub WorldMap: sector 'kokas1' (MAPS/kokas1.SEC) intra (col=21,row=13), raw rec 1b 01 84 00 00 00 (b1=0x01 TELEPORTAL, b5=0x00). NOTE: the KOFOR content was RELOCATED, not removed -- the old 'KOKAS + KOFOR' sector DQEV194225b was reorganized/renamed to 'kokas1' in the new map (correcting the earlier 'DQEV194225b removed -> KOFOR fully lost' note). Old-map pin was world(84,206,b). Name KOFOR VERIFIED-OBSERVED (sole KOFOR* roster name); name<->tile DERIVED (owner report + byte). The sibling HAV_COM (also in the old KOKAS+KOFOR sector) is likely in/near kokas1 too but stays STALE until walk-recorded -- do NOT guess it.

HAV_COM._evidence: [DERIVED binding / VERIFIED tile] RE-PINNED 2026-07-19 on the NEW final world map. Owner walk-recorded MEMORIZE on the HAV_COM teleportal at world(447,396,b). Byte-VERIFIED via the stub WorldMap: sector 'kokas1' (MAPS/kokas1.SEC) intra (col=31,row=10), raw rec 14 01 84 00 00 00 (b1=0x01 TELEPORTAL, b5=0x00). Confirms HAV_COM sits in the SAME sector as KOFOR (kokas1, the reorganized old 'KOKAS + KOFOR' / DQEV194225b) -- the geographic expectation held, but was still walk-recorded rather than guessed. Old-map pin was world(94,203,b). Name HAV_COM VERIFIED-OBSERVED (all 3 live dumps); name<->tile DERIVED (owner report + byte).

HAV_BANK._evidence: [DERIVED binding / VERIFIED tile] Labeled 2026-07-20 with the SEC teleportal labeler. Name is VERIFIED-OBSERVED (reference/dumps/teleporter_names.json); name<->tile binding is DERIVED (human map/geography judgement, NOT a live walk-record). dest LOST (.MSG content layer). REM_1._evidence: [DERIVED binding / VERIFIED tile] Live-recorded 2026-07-19 on the NEW final world map: owner MEMORIZE'd on the REM_1 teleportal at world(308,433,b). Byte-VERIFIED via the stub WorldMap: sector 'rem1' (MAPS/rem1.SEC) intra (col=20,row=15), raw rec 17 01 01 00 00 00 (b1=0x01 TELEPORTAL, b5=0x00). Name REM_1 VERIFIED-OBSERVED (in the live TP dumps, reference/dumps/teleporter_names.json); the sector filename 'rem1' corroborates. REM = 'Ruins of East Minton' [RECOVERED verbatim, wiki misc:glossary]; REM_2/REM_3 are sibling teleportals (roster, distinct cells, NOT pinned). level_req 10 per gameplay:travel ('REM_1 TP 10', MD/TELEPORTAL.md §8 / critter-aux-datamine), DOMAIN/DOC. name<->tile DERIVED (owner report + byte).

_recall: [DOMAIN/DOC destination + RE-AUTHORED landing cell] RECALL (C2S 0x5a, argument-less, dead-only self-rescue; costs a further 1% Exp+TRP) revives the player at 'the church in Haven' minus all non-personalized gear (ognall/MANUAL.TXT:2170-2185; ognall/HELP.TXT:994-1005; wiki gameplay:commands). Pointing it at the HAV_CHURCH tile above is the reconstruction decision (RE-AUTHORED): the docs name the church, not a cell, and landing ON the church teleportal is the closest sourced anchor. Opcode note: 0x5a is VERIFIED on the 12.5h CMDList @0x0043f480; the RECALL keyword is present in the 15.3 sweep @~0x44a320 but its 15.3 header byte is unread (OPEN-to-confirm, MD/TELEPORTAL.md §6).
_map_change_2026_07_19: The NEW FINAL world_map.json (310 sectors, coord space up to y~993) + updated MAPS replaced the earlier 117-sector community layout. ALL absolute world coords changed: e.g. HAV_CHURCH moved world(104,187,b) -> world(456,379,b) (same sector EWGB162193b, same intra col=8/row=25; the sector was just repositioned to block 14,11). Per owner 'wire only HAV_CHURCH for now', only HAV_CHURCH was re-pinned; HAV_INN/KOFOR/HAV_COM were NULLED to STALE (see their _evidence) and must be re-recorded on the live client. NOTE: this registry's HAV_CHURCH coord is only valid against the new world_map.json (uncommitted WIP at time of writing) -- commit the new map alongside.
_haven_sector_scan_OLD_MAP: STALE (pre-new-map, coords no longer valid; kept for reference). Both OLD Haven ground sectors raw-scanned 2026-07-16 for b1==1: EWGB162193b had 6 cells incl. intra (8,25)=old-world(104,187)=HAV_CHURCH; EWGB194225b had 6 cells incl. intra (3,6)=old-world(99,200)=HAV_INN. All byte-5==0x00. To pin the remaining unnamed cells now: walk the live client onto each tile on the NEW map and record its new world coord. CAUTION (learned 2026-07-19): HAV_* names are NOT all inside the Haven sectors (HAV_COM was in KOKAS+KOFOR); teleportal names do not track sector location — never bind by geography/elimination, only by an owner walk-record.
_level_table_DOMAIN_DOC: see MD/TELEPORTAL.md §8 — the gameplay:travel level gates; reference only, NOT a tile binding
